seoTitle: Resize a Table in Confluence: 2026 Guide
metaDescription: Learn how to resize a table in Confluence in 2026, switching between center, wide, and full-width modes and dragging column handles for balanced grids.
intro: A table that spills off the edge of the screen or squishes text into cramped cells hurts readability. This guide shows how to resize a Confluence table in 2026, both its overall width and individual column widths.

steps.heading: Open the table
steps.body: Click the pencil icon to enter edit mode and click inside your table.

steps.heading: Reveal the sizing toolbar
steps.body: Move your cursor onto the top edge table control strip to reveal the sizing toolbar.

steps.heading: Switch width modes
steps.body: Click the width adjustment icons to toggle between center, wide, and full-width page modes.

steps.heading: Resize individual columns
steps.body: Hover between two columns until the cursor changes, then drag the vertical handles to scale individual widths.

steps.heading: Balance columns evenly
steps.body: Open the advanced options and check the box for proportional sizing to balance all columns evenly.

steps.heading: Review and publish
steps.body: Confirm text, macros, and images wrap nicely inside the resized boundaries, then click Publish.

faq.question: Can I make a single table span the full width of the page?
faq.answer: Yes, the width adjustment icons let you toggle between center, wide, and complete full-width modes.

faq.question: How do I resize just one column instead of the whole table?
faq.answer: Hover between two columns until the cursor changes to a resize icon, then drag the vertical handle to scale that column.

faq.question: Can I lock a column's width so long text doesn't compress it?
faq.answer: Yes, overriding the automated column compression with a fixed-width setting prevents lengthy strings from forcing ugly vertical rows.

faq.question: Will resizing look consistent across screen sizes?
faq.answer: Yes, the rendering engine coordinates fractional cell widths so grids adapt cleanly across desktop and mobile layouts.
